title: triticum dicoccum subsp. atratum
intro: Triticum dicoccum subsp. atratum, also known as wild einkorn, is a subspecies of ancient grain that belongs to the wheat family. It is believed to have medicinal properties, with studies showing that it contains antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of wild einkorn include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and providing relief from digestive issues, among others. Herbal preparations of wild einkorn can be made into teas, infusions, or supplements, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
